LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF TAMARA THOMAS'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR DISMISSAL OF ACTION. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 27, 2026, AND THE COURT ORDERS SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING. LABOR CODE 2699, SUBDIVISION (S)(2), PROVIDES THAT "THE SUPERIOR COURT SHALL REVIEW AND APPROVE ANY SETTLEMENT OF ANY CIVIL ACTION FILED PURSUANT TO THIS PART. THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT SHALL BE SUBMITTED TO THE AGENCY AT THE SAME TIME THAT IT IS SUBMITTED TO THE COURT." THIS COURT'S VIEW IS THAT SETTLEMENT OF A CIVIL ACTION FILED PURSUANT TO PAGA, EVEN ONE THAT IS SUBSEQUENTLY SETTLED ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS, REQUIRES COURT APPROVAL AND LWDA SUBMISSION. IN PARTICULAR, THE MOVING PARTY MUST PROVIDE INFORMATION FROM WHICH THE COURT CAN DISCERN THAT THE RESOLUTION IS FAIR TO ABSENT AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES. THAT THEY CAN FILE THEIR OWN CLAIMS BECAUSE THEIR DISMISSAL IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE IS NOT SUFFICIENT. PLAINTIFF MAY PROVIDE ADDITIONAL FACTS SHOWING THAT THE RESOLUTION IS FAIR TO ABSENT AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES IN A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF FILED NO LATER THAN FIVE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, JAN-27-2026 RE: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE AND UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR DISMISSAL OF ACTION. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR DISMISSAL OF ACTION IS GRANTED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA) ***AFTER HEARING AN ORDER IS EXECUTED.

Notice And Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Action
Off Calendar Jan-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF TAMARA THOMAS', AN INDIVIDUAL, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL PERSONS SIMILARLY SITUATED Notice And Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Action.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion for dismissal of action is granted.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice And Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Action
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Continued To Jan-27-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TAMARA THOMAS'S Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Action.

The motion is continued to January 27, 2026, and the court orders supplemental briefing.

Labor Code 2699, subdivision (s)(2), provides that "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part. The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." This court's view is that settlement of a civil action filed pursuant to PAGA, even one that is subsequently settled on an individual basis, requires court approval and LWDA submission. In particular, the moving party must provide information from which the court can discern that the resolution is fair to absent aggrieved employees. That they can file their own claims because their dismissal is without prejudice is not sufficient.

Plaintiff may provide additional facts showing that the resolution is fair to absent aggrieved employees in a supplemental brief filed no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
